description: Opportunity-score weights, per-dimension component weights, effort/ROI bands, amplifier/suppressor caps, conviction rules, and lifecycle rules. Loop 7 (scoring) and Loop 8 (amplify/suppress) read this. Tuning these adjusts scoring without touching subagent code.

opportunity_weights._doc: OpportunityScore = sum(dimension_score * weight). Must sum to 1.0. Matches the mission spec EXACTLY: Relevance 30%, Audience Match 25%, Reach 15%, Growth Signals 15%, Ease of Access 10%, Cost Efficiency 5%.

tiers._doc: The mission says only surface opportunities above 70, so the opportunity_floor is 70. Everything below goes to the watchlist.

dimension_components._doc: Each dimension score = clamp(sum(component_value * weight) * source_reliability_multiplier, 0, 100). Component weights per dimension sum to 1.0. Components marked inverse:true mean a HIGH raw observation LOWERS the score (e.g. high cost vs budget => low cost_efficiency).

effort_bands._doc: Set in Loop 7 from ease_of_access + cost_efficiency + the brief budget. Surfaced as an effort badge and used by the quick-win-first sort.
effort_bands.EFFORT_LOW: Apply/DM/post today; little or no spend; published contact + open to promo.
effort_bands.EFFORT_MED: Outreach + negotiation needed; modest spend within budget.
effort_bands.EFFORT_HIGH: Contract/retainer/long lead time OR spend near/over budget.

roi_bands._doc: expected_roi is a coarse band derived from (reach * audience_match * relevance) / max(est_cost, floor). Owned/free channels and high-fit low-cost plays land HIGH.
roi_bands.HIGH: Strong fit + reach for low/no cost, or a confirmed high-converting channel.
roi_bands.LOW: Weak fit-to-cost ratio or cost strains the budget (kept only if reach/relevance very high).

amplifiers._doc: Loop 8 awards +per_signal for each INDEPENDENT signal type firing, capped at max_amplifier. convergence_count = number of distinct signal types fired (0-8).

suppressors._doc: Loop 8 subtracts per_flag for each vanity/no-substance flag, capped at max_suppressor (a negative number).

conviction._doc: Independent of the score. How much to trust the score, from independent_source_count + recency + promotion-history evidence. The mission's 'prefer high-conviction over large-but-speculative' rule is enforced by the report's conviction sort and a tier cap on LOW conviction.

rules.HIGH: independent_source_count >= 3 AND at least one recent (within Y..Y-2) confirmation AND core reach verified

conviction._doc_cap: An opportunity with LOW conviction (or an Unverified core reach claim) cannot exceed the QUALIFIED tier, no matter the raw score.

lifecycle._doc: Derived in Loop 10 from the score trend in score_history plus any user-logged outreach_status. delta = score(this cycle) - score(prior cycle). User-logged outreach status is never overwritten by discovery.

rules.stale: no fresh evidence for 2 consecutive cycles (data_age over TTL) OR dropped below floor
rules.contacted_candidate: user has logged an outreach_status of contacted/in_progress (takes precedence as a display state)

budget_allocation._doc: Loop 10 uses these defaults to draft the Recommended Marketing Budget Allocation, then adjusts to the brief's actual budget and the surfaced opportunities. These are starting splits, not hard rules; the plan explains every deviation.
